summary: Started decompiling and documenting the mnvibration menu module. The PR added substantial C implementation for controller rumble toggles, player/name vibration settings UI, JObj/text setup and cleanup, and menu lifecycle handling. It also replaced many placeholder header declarations with typed prototypes and exposed the data/float globals needed by the implementation. The author explicitly marked the work as WIP and said comments/preliminary documentation would need stronger validation in follow-up PRs. decomp.dev reported 5 new full matches and 12 partial improvements in main/melee/mn/mnvibration, for +1132 matched bytes and +0.03% overall matched code.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/mn/mnvibration.c by roughly 944 added lines, replacing a very small stub-like file with substantial mnvibration logic.
Added includes for ft, gm, lb, mn, baselib, Dolphin OS, controller, JObj, GObj, memory, and SisLib dependencies used by the menu implementation.
Added local inline JObj translation getter/setter helpers named mnVibration_JObjGetTranslationX/Y/Z and mnVibration_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z, including assert patterns and JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT handling.
Added static/global data including mnVibration_804D4FE8, mnVibration_804D4FF0, mnVibration_804D6C28, and multiple MnVibrationJointAssets globals.

Introduced local structs for MnVibrationJointAssets and MnVibrationData, with field comments for controller scroll state, connected flags, scroll offset, 25 JObj references, 8 text handles, title text, and cursor gobj.

Implemented or partially implemented mnvibration functions including mnVibration_802474C4, fn_80247510, fn_80248084, mnVibration_802480B4, mnVibration_8024829C, mnVibration_80248444, mnVibration_80248644, fn_80248748, fn_802487A8, fn_80248A78, and mnVibration_80248ED4.
Added menu input behavior for B-button exit, A-button rumble toggles, controller-specific rumble state changes, up/down navigation through saved names, cursor movement, and scroll offset handling.
Added text lifecycle handling for name-list entries and title text using HSD_SisLib allocation/display/free functions.

Added JObj asset loading and animation setup for controller panels and name-row rumble indicators using HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_JObjAddChild, and HSD_JObjRemoveAll.
Changed mnVibration_80249174 to save arg0 in a local variable and call typed mnVibration_80248ED4(saved_arg) instead of casting around a header mismatch.
Updated src/melee/mn/mnvibration.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to concrete return types and parameter types for the mnvibration functions.
Added header declarations for MnVibrationFloatData, mnVibration_803EECE0, mnVibration_804DC020, mnVibration_803EED04, mnVibration_804D4FF4, and mnVibration_804D4FFC.
smart_moves: Mirrored a known ftCo_800C6AFC-style JObj setter pattern locally, including assert line constants and matrix invalidation behavior, which likely helped match call shape around direct translate.x/y/z access.
Kept asset bundles as small typed structs with joint/animjoint/matanim/shapeanim fields, making repeated JObj creation code easier to express and reuse.
Modeled menu instance state in MnVibrationData instead of scattering raw offsets, while preserving enough offset comments to support matching and later validation.
Converted placeholder header prototypes to typed signatures, which removed the need for an unsafe function-pointer cast in mnVibration_80249174.
Reloaded mnVibration_804D6C28->user_data during B-button exit cleanup before freeing text objects, which is a cautious pattern when global menu state may be involved.
Used explicit cleanup paths for SisLib text objects, GObj unlinking, and pad rumble removal on menu exit.
Separated list rebuilding into mnVibration_80248644: it frees old text, removes old row JObjs, and repopulates up to 8 visible names from scroll_offset.
decomp_lessons: For menu modules built around HSD_JObj trees, define local data structs with known JObj array counts and text-handle arrays early; even partial field names make input, animation, and cleanup code tractable.
When matching direct JObj translate access, local inline wrappers can preserve assert strings/line numbers and matrix-dirty side effects while avoiding repeated boilerplate.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with real prototypes can unlock cleaner callsites and avoid cast hacks that obscure matching problems.

Menu decomp often requires reproducing small state-machine fields exactly: cooldown checks, animation frame counters, controller selection bits, cursor row, and scroll offset all affect control flow shape.
Name-list UI code should treat 0xFF as an invalid name index sentinel when visible rows exceed GetNameCount or scroll_offset points past the available data.
Text object cleanup and JObj child removal are important in scrollable menu rebuilds; leaks or stale children can hide matching mistakes as well as runtime bugs.
Controller connection checks in this code use HSD_PadCopyStatus[i].err to decide whether a panel is active.
The PR body warns that comments and documentation were preliminary; future work should validate field names and comments against more context before treating them as final.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based symbol names for unmatched or not-yet-renamed functions, e.g. mnVibration_802474C4, fn_80247510, fn_80248748, fn_80248A78, and mnVibration_80248ED4.
Used module-prefixed local helper names for JObj wrappers: mnVibration_JObjGetTranslationX/Y/Z and mnVibration_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z.
Used module-prefixed structs: MnVibrationAssets, MnVibrationJointAssets, MnVibrationData, and MnVibrationFloatData.
Kept unknown or semi-known struct fields in offset-style names such as x0, x6, x18, x1C, while documenting likely purpose in comments.
Global data retained address-style names such as mnVibration_804A0868, mnVibration_804A0878, mnVibration_804D6C28, mnVibration_803EECE0, and mnVibration_804DC020.
Generic function names beginning with fn_ were retained where semantic names were not yet validated.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used local inline JObj get/set helpers to match direct field loads/stores, assert calls, and ftCo_800C6AFC invalidation behavior.
Inserted PAD_STACK markers in several functions to preserve stack layout where needed.
Kept raw integer constants and frame/button masks visible, such as inputs & 2 for B, inputs & 1 for A, inputs & 0x40/0x80 and repeated-input masks 0x10/0x20.
Used explicit NULL child/next traversal patterns for JObj children instead of abstracting traversal, preserving branch structure.
Used do/while loops for fixed-count operations over controllers, text slots, and JObj arrays, matching common compiler output patterns.
Added local saved_arg in mnVibration_80249174 to support a typed call to mnVibration_80248ED4 while preserving argument value.
Represented float data through MnVibrationFloatData and cast/field access around mnVibration_803EECE0 for animation frame values.
decomp.dev evidence: 5 functions reached 100% match: mnVibration_802480B4, mnVibration_8024829C, fn_80248748, mnVibration_802474C4, and fn_80248084.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body says the work was still WIP and that preliminary documentation/comments would be more strongly validated in follow-up PRs.
decomp.dev reported +1132 matched bytes, 5 new full matches, and 12 improved unmatched items in main/melee/mn/mnvibration.

Partial-match improvements reported by decomp.dev included fn_80247510 at 73.73%, fn_80248A78 at 97.97%, fn_802487A8 at 87.42%, mnVibration_80248ED4 at 81.58%, mnVibration_80248444 at 91.62%, and mnVibration_80248644 at 88.25%.
follow_up_queries: Validate MnVibrationData field names x0 and x6 against later mnvibration PRs or runtime behavior.
Check follow-up PRs for semantic renames of fn_80247510, fn_80248748, fn_802487A8, and fn_80248A78.
Confirm whether the preliminary comments for controller scroll states, connected flags, and name-list rows survived later review.
Investigate remaining unmatched percentages for fn_80247510, fn_80248A78, fn_802487A8, mnVibration_80248ED4, mnVibration_80248444, and mnVibration_80248644.
Look for shared baselib JObj translation inline helpers that could replace module-local wrappers without regressing matches.
